metadata.title: merged — technical screening without interviews
metadata.description: Instead of a Leetcode interview — one calibrated task in a real repo. The candidate opens a PR, we score it automatically against a rubric. AI is allowed — tasks are designed so it's necessary but not sufficient.

hero.subtitle: Instead of Leetcode — one calibrated task in a real repo. The candidate opens a pull request. The system scores it automatically: tests, diff shape, commit quality, responses to review.

problem.title: Technical interviews are broken. Everyone knows it, and everyone keeps doing them.
problem.body1BehavioralSuffix: — the skill of telling STAR stories. None of them show how a person actually works day to day.
problem.body2: And in 2026 even that illusion of signal is gone: Copilot and Cursor close a typical task in 10 minutes. Your seniors run dozens of screening calls a month and dread how much time this stage burns.
problem.footnote: * Cost estimate — screening one candidate, including engineer time

howItWorks.title: Four steps. Zero hours of engineering time.

steps.title: Recruiter picks a task
steps.body: From the bank — matched to the candidate's level (Junior / Middle / Senior) and stack. No call, no whiteboard. 30 seconds in the panel.

steps.title: Candidate opens a pull request
steps.body: Gets a private repo with real context. AI is allowed — tasks are designed so it's necessary but not sufficient.

steps.title: System scores it automatically
steps.body: CI tests, diff focus, commit quality, responses to auto-review. An LLM judge reads the entire PR against a structured rubric.

steps.title: Recruiter sees a ranked report
steps.body: Rubric scores, link to the PR, strengths and weaknesses. After that — just a final interview with the team for culture fit.

levels.title: The task is calibrated to the level.
levels.subtitle: The product's moat is task design. We don't fight AI — we design tasks so that without understanding the system, AI is just a typewriter. Every task is hand-calibrated on live candidates.
levels.note: AI is allowed and expected. A "blind" Claude solution scores 30–40/100: tests fail on edge cases, the PR description is empty, commits are one big blob. The rubric measures understanding of the system, not the fact that code got written.

levels.hook: Add a feature to a clean repo
levels.desc: A small project with its own conventions. You have to read the README, avoid breaking anything else, and write a test. Cursor can handle this — we're filtering out people who can't.

levels.hook: Reproduce a bug and fix it
levels.desc: A larger repo, vaguely phrased task: "users complain that Y behaves oddly in case Z." AI doesn't know what to fix — you have to localize the root cause.
Task: "ship this feature so that in six months it can be extended to W without a rewrite." A design doc in the PR is required — AI will write the code, but it won't make the decisions for you.

signals.title: "Green CI" is only 20% of the signal.
signals.subtitle: The other 80% is what an LLM judge does better than a human interviewer when it has a structured rubric: reads the whole PR, the description, the commit history, and the responses to comments. No fatigue, no bias.

rubric.heading: Rubric weights
rubric.footnote: * Weights are configurable at the task template level

llm.label: Rationale in the PR description
llm.note: Whether the "why" is explained, not just the "what"

forWhom.title: The developer is the user. The hiring manager is the buyer.
forWhom.subtitle: Each role gets what matters: developers get a fair async evaluation without live coding; managers get their team's time back and a sharper final round.

lines: You're not coding under stress for 45 minutes — you take the task and work at your own pace.
AI assistants aren't banned — they're expected. Your usual Cursor / Claude / Copilot setup just works.
You see the rubric up front: what's being scored, which signals matter. No vibe check.

metrics.label: returned to the team each month

lines: Your team doesn't burn 40 hours a month on screening calls.
The final meeting is about the person, not technical basics.
You see how the candidate thinks and justifies decisions — not just what they wrote.

objections.title: Five things you're thinking right now.

items.q: What if the candidate just hands the task to Claude?
items.a: Let them. Tasks are designed so a "blind" AI solution scores 30–40 out of 100: tests fail on edge cases, the PR description is empty, commits are one big blob, responses to auto-review are generic. We don't measure "wrote code" — we measure "understood the system."

items.q: Tasks will leak onto the internet and into model training data.
items.a: Every task is parameterized: one template yields dozens of variants with different seeds, names, and emphases in the requirements. A public solution to one specific version won't pass another. Plus — an option for private tasks on your own code on the enterprise plan.

items.q: A senior won't do a three-hour take-home.
items.a: Agreed. For seniors — a 45-minute paired session: the candidate shares their screen, solves alongside AI, and the system records telemetry (how long they thought, what they searched, how much they rewrote). It fits on a calendar and measures more than a classic interview.

items.q: This doesn't replace the final interview with the team, does it?
items.a: We're not removing the interview. We're removing the stage where you burn 40 engineering hours filtering out people who simply can't code. Culture fit and "do I want to work with them for five years" stays a live conversation.

items.q: How is this better than HackerRank / CodeSignal / Codility?
items.a: They measure Leetcode — in 2026. We measure real work: a PR into a real repo with real context, scored by an LLM judge against a rubric. A different product category — work-sample assessment for the AI era.

privacy.metaDescription: merged collects the minimum data required for screening. Servers are in the EU (AWS eu-central-1). Data deletion requests — request@merged.com.ua.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.body1: merged collects the minimum data required to run the service: email, name, company, and — while a candidate is completing a task — metadata from the candidate's PR. We do not share personal data with third parties. Servers are in the EU (Frankfurt, AWS eu-central-1).

terms.metaDescription: merged terms of use. Closed beta, service provided "as is," no fees charged. The customer is responsible for obtaining candidate consent to data processing.
terms.heading: Terms of Use
terms.body1: Closed beta. The service is provided "as is." No fees are charged during the beta period. The customer is responsible for the legal correctness of the hiring process on their side (candidate consent to data processing, absence of discrimination, and so on).
